findings.evidence: `rg "intersectionForm_self_eq_dotProduct|theorem intersectionForm_self" code/TauCeti/LowDimTopology/Plumbing` shows the new lemma only in `NegativeDefinite.lean`, while `IntersectionForm.lean` already defines `intersectionForm_apply` and `intersectionForm_self` as the base self-pairing API.
findings.fix: Move `intersectionForm_self_eq_dotProduct` to `TauCeti/LowDimTopology/Plumbing/IntersectionForm.lean`, near `intersectionForm_apply` or `intersectionForm_self`, and import/use it from `NegativeDefinite.lean`.
findings.issue: `PlumbingGraph.intersectionForm_self_eq_dotProduct` is generic `intersectionForm`/`intersectionMatrix` API and has no negative-definiteness hypothesis, so placing it in `NegativeDefinite.lean` hides a basic conversion lemma from users of `IntersectionForm.lean` and invites duplication.

summary: One general intersection-form lemma is placed in the negative-definite extension even though it belongs with the base form API. The negative-definiteness consequences themselves are in the natural plumbing module and the imports are not evidently wrong.
